hero.description: FluxMedia brings the generation models supported by the current system into one creative entry point, so a sentence can reliably become work you can keep using.
hero.prompt: Distant mountains unfolding through mist, drawn from language into ink.
hero.mainAlt: FluxMedia-generated artwork of a bold ink circle on open rice paper
hero.draftAlt: A pale ink draft from the generation process of the same artwork
hero.depthAlt: Close detail of ink fibers and dry-brush texture

models.title: Image models available now.
models.description: This catalog comes from the running configuration and only shows public image models the platform can currently serve. For any account, the models callable by an API Key are always determined by its GET /v1/models response.
models.unavailable: The runtime model catalog is currently unavailable. Please try again later.

image.label: Image
image.description: For text-to-image, conversational image creation, and supported image workflows.
image.empty: No public image model is currently available to display.

video.label: Video
video.description: For video generation paths currently connected to the system.
video.empty: No public video model is currently available to display.

conversation.label: Conversation
conversation.description: Current models for conversation and Responses capabilities.
conversation.empty: No public conversation model is currently available to display.

artworks.title: Real generations. Real space.
artworks.description: These works are existing FluxMedia-generated samples cleared for public display. They unfold in the normal page flow, without requiring a long animation to reveal them.

alts.bamboo: East Asian ink artwork of pale bamboo shadows and open space
alts.mountain: Ink landscape of distant mountain layers receding into mist
alts.plum: Minimal ink artwork of plum branches and blossoms
alts.waterfall: Ink landscape of a waterfall descending through mountains
alts.carp: Ink artwork of two carp moving through water
alts.lotus: Ink artwork of lotus leaves and blossoms in open space
alts.bridge: Ink landscape joining an arched bridge to a distant view
alts.rain: Ink landscape of sparse rain and distant trees
alts.circle: Generated artwork of a bold ink circle on rice paper

reliability.title: Reliability, limited to what we can verify.
reliability.description: Statistics come from the latest finished generations. Moderation stops and invalid requests are excluded from the platform availability denominator.
reliability.insufficient: There are not enough finished samples to calculate an availability percentage yet.
reliability.unavailable: Reliability statistics are currently unavailable, so no fixed percentage is shown here.
reliability.hiddenDescription: Visitors do not see reliability figures. Administrators can enable them here.

faq.title: What you may want to know before starting.
faq.description: Direct answers about current models, API integration, and the creative entry point.

items.question: Which models does FluxMedia currently support?
items.answer: The homepage catalog reads the current runtime image configuration. Configuration changes appear on following requests. Models callable by a specific account still come from GET /v1/models with that API Key.

items.question: Why does the homepage only show image models?
items.answer: The public site currently focuses on image generation, so both the model catalog and quick integration example only use runtime image models available for public display.

items.question: How do I integrate image generation through the API?
items.answer: Create an API Key in the console, call GET /v1/models from your server to confirm available models, then use the existing POST /v1/images/generations endpoint. Keep the API Key server-side; external API access depends on current account permissions.

items.question: How do I start creating?
items.answer: After signing in, open the creation workspace, describe the visual in natural language, and keep refining it. Visitors without an account can create one and continue through the same creative entry point.

items.question: Why might the reliability percentage be absent?
items.answer: The homepage only presents results the statistics service can verify. When display is disabled, the sample is insufficient, or loading fails, a fixed number is never substituted for real statistics.

footer.title: Your next work begins with your language.
footer.description: Enter the existing creation workspace and turn an idea into a visual work you can continue to edit, save, and use.
footer.brandDescription: From language to work, making models reliable creative infrastructure.

externalApi.title: API Keys
externalApi.description: Create and manage OpenAI-compatible FluxMedia API keys. External calls consume account credits and are recorded in usage and generation history.
externalApi.createSectionDescription: Create a separate key for a site or application, with an optional generation group and credit limit.
externalApi.listDescription: Expand an active key to edit its group and quota. Revoked keys are read-only.
externalApi.requiresStarter: Your current plan cannot create or edit API keys. Existing keys can still be revoked and deleted.
externalApi.newKeyLabel: New API key, shown only once
externalApi.copyFallback: If automatic copying fails, focus the field above and copy the text manually.
externalApi.confirmRevoke: Revoking “{name}” immediately stops authentication and cannot be undone.
externalApi.confirmDelete: Delete the revoked key “{name}”? This cannot be undone.
externalApi.revokedReadOnlyDescription: Its group and credit limit are locked. Delete the key permanently if it is no longer needed.
